reasoning: The US relies heavily on airstrikes and special operations forces (which are excluded from resolving this question positively) to conduct operations in Yemen, actively avoiding the deployment of regular US ground troops. Market sentiment as of late October 2025 was already very low (6%), and searches up to the November 9, 2025 cutoff reveal no changes: experts continue to view regular ground troop deployment as highly unlikely. With less than two months remaining in 2025 and no indications of imminent regular ground operations or mobilization for such, the probability should drift below the late-October market price. I estimate a 3% chance.
